name: Harry Belafonte
description: American singer, actor and civil rights activist (1927–2023)
abstract: Harry Belafonte ( BEL-ə-FON-tee; born Harold George Bellanfanti Jr.; March 1, 1927 – April 25, 2023) was an American singer, actor, and civil rights activist who popularized calypso music with international audiences in the 1950s and 1960s. Belafonte's career breakthrough album Calypso (1956) was the first million-selling LP by a single …

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: He did a Harry Belafonte act, guitar and played you know. He worked with Bobby, accompanied Bobby a little bit, this was before I was with Bobby.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: And the only example I can think of is Harry Belafonte, you know with “Matilda” and all that kind of stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: I ended up with Harry Belafonte. And when I got with him... And he was going to feature me. And what I found in performing with Harry Belafonte...
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: We worked together at the Apollo Theater. This was a very interesting lineup. The lineup was Harry Belafonte...
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: And I used to do, I did all the Belafonte dates, you know, recording sessions.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: I think, though I'm not sure if he still does, but Harry Belafonte lives in the same building. Lived, if not lives, lived in the same building as Lena Horne on this street, Western Avenue.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
